user_request: Prepare a detailed plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as a hand loan by bank transfer. There is no written agreement. The transaction is supported by bank statement and WhatsApp communications. The Defendant has failed to repay despite repeated oral demands and legal notice. Plead limitation properly. Claim interest at 12% per annum from date of loan till realization. Draft strictly in accordance with Order VII CPC. Include proper cause of action, jurisdiction, valuation, court fee and prayer clause. Use formal Indian court language suitable for filing before City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

facts.summary: Recovery of Rs. 15,00,000 advanced as a hand loan via bank transfer without a written agreement. The transaction is supported by bank statement and WhatsApp communications. The Defendant has failed to repay despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice.

chronology.event: Loan of Rs. 15,00,000 advanced via bank transfer
Repeated oral demands for repayment made
Defendant failed to repay despite notice

evidence.description: Evidence of the bank transfer for the loan

slots.value: From date of loan till realization

assumptions: Jurisdiction inferred as Bengaluru, Karnataka based on 'City Civil Court, Bengaluru'

missing_fields: Plaintiff and Defendant names and addresses
assumptions: Loan is within the 3-year limitation period from the date of cause of action or last acknowledgment.

Order 7, r 1 of the code prescribes what a plaint should contain, but no section or order or Rule of CPC lays down 
as to what a petition should contain. On the other hand the Code does not say that the ingredients required for 
plaint are the ingredients for a petition. Therefore, a petition is completely different from a plaint. Thus, a petition for 
divorce under s 13 of the Hindu Marriage Act, 1955 is not a plaint and a final order passed under the Act is not a 
decree under s 2 (2) of the CPC. Therefore, decree passed without separate prayer for consequential relief is 
enforceable and court-fees for divorce petition is not Rs. 20/- but Re. 1/- as prescribed for petition.33

jurisdictional purposes should be on the basis of annual rent or profit. Suit so valued along with the claim of

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint for Recovery of Money Under Order VII of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU
CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{YEAR}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
{{PLAINTIFF_AGE}}, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}
...                                             PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
{{DEFENDANT_AGE}}, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}
...                                             DEFENDANT

PLAINT UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

1. The Plaintiff is {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by profession, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. The Defendant is {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by profession, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} and is thus amenable to the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

2. The Plaintiff states that on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN}}, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant through bank transfer. No written agreement was executed between the parties in respect of the said loan. The transaction is evidenced by bank transfer records and subsequent WhatsApp communications wherein the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said amount and his liability to repay the same. A copy of the relevant bank statement is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A. Copies of the WhatsApp communications are annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B.

3. The said loan was advanced by the Plaintiff in good faith and upon the Defendant's promise to repay the same on demand or within a reasonable time. Despite the absence of a written instrument, a valid oral contract of loan was constituted, and the Defendant is bound to repay the principal amount together with interest thereon.

4. That the Plaintiff has made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant for repayment of the said loan amount, particulars whereof are {{DATE_OF_ORAL_DEMANDS}}, but the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the same.

5. That thereafter, the Plaintiff, through his Advocate, caused a legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} to be served upon the Defendant calling upon him to repay the outstanding amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- together with interest within the time stipulated therein. The Defendant received the said notice but failed to make any payment towards the discharge of his liability. Copy of the legal notice along with dispatch receipt/acknowledgment is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure C.

6. CAUSE OF ACTION:
(a) The cause of action first arose on {{DATE_OF_LOAN}} when the Plaintiff transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant as an oral/hand loan.
(b) The right to sue accrued on {{DATE_OF_DEMAND_REFUSAL}} when the Defendant failed and refused to repay the amount despite oral demands and subsequently upon failure to comply with the legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}}.
(c) The cause of action is not of a continuing nature, save and except that the Defendant's liability to pay interest continues to accrue till the date of realization.

7. LIMITATION:
The present suit is not barred by limitation under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years for suits "For money payable for money lent" commencing from the date when the loan is made. The Plaintiff submits that the suit has been filed within the prescribed period of three years from the date when the right to sue accrued.

8. JURISDICTION:
(a) Pecuniary Jurisdiction: The value of the subject-matter of the suit is Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) plus interest thereon, which is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon'ble City Civil Court.
(b) Territorial Jurisdiction: The Defendant resides/carries on business within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and/or the cause of action in whole or in part arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

9. VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
The suit is valued for the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fees at Rs. {{SUIT_VALUATION}}/- (Rupees {{SUIT_VALUATION_WORDS}} only), comprising the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- and accrued interest thereon as pleaded hereinabove. The ad valorem court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on the said valuation in accordance with the provisions of the Karnataka Court-fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

10. The Plaintiff has not filed any other suit in respect of the cause of action hereinbefore pleaded.

PRAYER:
Wherefore, in the facts and circumstances aforesaid, the Plaintiff most humbly prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only);
(b) Award interest @ 12% per annum on the said principal amount from the date of loan, that is, {{DATE_OF_LOAN}}, till the date of realization;
(c) Award costs of this suit to the Plaintiff;
(d) Pass such other order or direction as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

Place: Bengaluru
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

VERIFICATION:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify and state on solemn affirmation as follows:

1. The contents of paras 1 to 3 of the preceding plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge and belief.
2. The contents of para 4 are based on {{SOURCE_OF_KNOWLEDGE}} and believed to be true.
3. The contents of para 5 are true and correct based on the records of my Advocate and the postal receipts.
4. The contents of paras 6 to 10 and the prayer clause are based on legal advice and believed to be true.

Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}} day of {{MONTH}} {{YEAR}}.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
... PLAINTIFF

LIST OF DOCUMENTS
(As required under Order VII Rule 14 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

1. Annexure A: Bank Statement evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant;
2. Annexure B: WhatsApp Communications showing acknowledgment of debt and request for repayment;
3. Annexure C: Legal Notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} along with dispatch receipt and/or acknowledgment card.

Place: Bengaluru
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
... PLAINTIFF

blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action anchored to a procedural step rather than the date when the right to sue actually accrued under the applicable law
blocking_issues.fix: Identify the correct accrual date from FACTS based on the cause of action type, and use that as the cause of action date; procedural steps are not accrual events. Under Article 19 of the Limitation Act, 1963 (for money lent), the right to sue accrues when the loan is made, not upon refusal after demand. Remove the reference to right to suit accruing on the date of refusal/non-payment.
